You are implementing FL-08 in a BIM web app (monorepo at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai). Start with: git checkout main && git pull origin main ## What already exists (do NOT rewrite these) - `packages/web/src/workspace/materialLayerCatalogWorkbench.ts` — logic: resolveMaterialLayerReadout, buildUpsertLayeredTypeCommand, validateLayerAuthoringDraft, materialRowsToDraft, LayerAuthoringDraftRow, BUILTIN_MATERIAL_DISPLAY, etc. - `packages/web/src/workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx` — renders the layer table; editing cells works for wall_type/floor_type/roof_type instances; has an "Apply type stack" button that calls onUpsertSemantic(buildUpsertLayeredTypeCommand(...)) — LIMITATION: no way to add or remove rows (fix this in Task B below) - `packages/web/src/workspace/AuthoringWorkbenchesPanel.tsx` — mounts MaterialLayerStackWorkbench; already wired into RedesignedWorkspace ## Three tasks --- ### Task A — "Types" section in the left-rail browser File: `packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx` Read the `browserSections` useMemo (around line 740). It already has sections: project, views, sheets, schedules, families. Add a new section after 'schedules' and before 'families': ```ts { id: 'types', label: 'Types', rows: [ { id: 'wall-types', label: 'Wall Types', children: (Object.values(elementsById) as Element[]) .filter((e): e is Extract => e.kind === 'wall_type') .map((t) => ({ id: t.id, label: t.name, hint: `${t.layers.length} layers` })), }, { id: 'floor-types', label: 'Floor Types', children: (Object.values(elementsById) as Element[]) .filter((e): e is Extract => e.kind === 'floor_type') .map((t) => ({ id: t.id, label: t.name, hint: `${t.layers.length} layers` })), }, { id: 'roof-types', label: 'Roof Types', children: (Object.values(elementsById) as Element[]) .filter((e): e is Extract => e.kind === 'roof_type') .map((t) => ({ id: t.id, label: t.name, hint: `${t.layers.length} layers` })), }, ].filter((g) => g.children.length > 0), }, Clicking a type row in the browser should select it (set selectedId). The existing onRowActivate handler already calls setSelectedId(id) for unknown ids — check that it falls through to that default for wall_type/floor_type/roof_type ids. Also add a "New type" button row to each group. Use an action hint or a separate onNewType callback — look at how FL-06 added custom family types and follow the same pattern. The button should call: void onSemanticCommand({ type: 'upsertWallType', // or upsertFloorType / upsertRoofType name: 'New Wall Type', basisLine: 'center', layers: [{ thicknessMm: 200, function: 'structure', materialKey: '' }], }) Use crypto.randomUUID() as the id if the backend requires one (check the test file app/tests/test_material_assembly_schedule.py line ~69 to see if id is required or optional in the command — if optional, omit it). --- Task B — Add/remove rows in MaterialLayerStackWorkbench File: packages/web/src/workspace/MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx In the typeAuthoring block (where draftRows.length rows are rendered and "Apply type stack" button exists), add: Add row button (below the table, before Apply): Remove row button (as a last column in each row): Add a header cell for the remove column, and in each add: --- Task C — Type picker in wall/floor/roof inspector File: packages/web/src/workspace/InspectorContent.tsx The InspectorPropertiesFor function already accepts an options parameter with elementsById and onPropertyChange (added in GW-05). In the case 'wall': block, below the existing roof attachment select, add a wall type picker:
{f('wallType')}
Similarly for case 'floor': — add a floorTypeId picker using floor_type elements. Similarly for case 'roof': — add a roofTypeId picker using roof_type elements. Also add i18n keys to packages/web/src/i18n.ts (both en and de blocks): wallType: 'Wall Type', // DE: 'Wandtyp' floorType: 'Floor Type', // DE: 'Deckentyp' roofType: 'Roof Type', // DE: 'Dachtyp' --- Verification pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web typecheck pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --run Both must pass with 0 errors. Branch & commit Branch: fl-08-type-catalog-ui Commit: "feat(ui): FL-08 — wall/floor/roof type catalog browser + layer authoring" Push to origin.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
All three tasks done. Typecheck: 0 errors. Tests: 1181/1181 passed. Branch pushed.

**Summary of changes:**

- **Task A** (`RedesignedWorkspace.tsx`): Added a `types` browser section with Wall/Floor/Roof Type groups (children include existing type rows with layer count hints plus a `+ New *` row). The `onRowActivate` handler intercepts `new-wall-type`, `new-floor-type`, `new-roof-type` IDs and fires `upsertWallType`/`upsertFloorType`/`upsertRoofType` commands with `crypto.randomUUID()`. Existing wall/floor/roof type element IDs fall through to `select(id)`.

- **Task B** (`MaterialLayerStackWorkbench.tsx`): Added a `×` remove button as a last column in each draft row (reindexes remaining rows), and a `+ Add layer` button above "Apply type stack".

- **Task C** (`InspectorContent.tsx` + `i18n.ts`): Added `wallTypeId` picker in the `wall` case, `floorTypeId` picker in the `floor` case, and `roofTypeId` picker in the `roof` case — all filtered from `elementsById`. Added `wallType`/`floorType`/`roofType` keys in both EN and DE translation blocks.
